# Restore Memory Core boot-time auto-summarization (startup.summarizationStatus = not_attempted)

## Context

Surfaced 2026-05-01 during the Memory Core diagnostic that produced #10556 (`fix(ai): backfill ChromaDB legacy userId`). Healthcheck consistently reports:

```json
"startup": {
  "summarizationStatus": "not_attempted",
  "summarizationDetails": null
}
```

Per AGENTS_STARTUP.md §6: *"On startup, the Memory Core server automatically finds and summarizes any previous sessions that were not yet processed."*

This is NOT happening. New sessions accumulate raw memories without ever being summarized into the `neo-agent-sessions` collection. This is independent of the tenant-isolation rollout (#10556) — the boot-summarization daemon should produce new tagged summaries for current tenants, but isn't running.

## The Problem

Two distinct symptoms cluster here, both observable today:

1. **`startup.summarizationStatus: not_attempted`** — the boot-time auto-summarization promised by AGENTS_STARTUP.md §6 never fires.
2. **`runSandman` produces no `sandman_handoff.md`** — the Golden Path / strategic-roadmap synthesis depends on summaries to build the topological prioritization. Without summarization, no handoff.

Cluster of related downstream effects:
- Agents querying their own past sessions get nothing back (covered by #10556's read-path fix once migration runs)
- The Dream Pipeline / REM cycle has no incremental summary corpus to ingest
- Strategic context-priming via `get_context_frontier` returns empty (also tracked by the strategicNeighbors guard fix in #10557 → #10558, but the deeper issue is no summaries to weight)

## The Architectural Reality

The Memory Core's summarization pipeline lives across:

- `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/SessionSummarization.spec.mjs` — existing test scaffolding (suggests the pipeline IS implemented)
- Boot-time invocation likely in `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/Server.mjs` or `services/lifecycle/SystemLifecycleService.mjs`
- Sandman daemon: `buildScripts/ai/runSandman.mjs` (separate concern; produces `sandman_handoff.md`)
- Healthcheck observability surface: `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/HealthService.mjs` — `#startupSummarizationStatus` private field + `startup.summarizationStatus` payload field already exposed; population path is what's broken

Two failure modes possible:

1. **Pipeline is wired but never invoked at boot.** The `#startupSummarizationStatus` field stays at its default `null` because no code path calls the summarization on boot.
2. **Pipeline is invoked but fails silently.** The `summarizationDetails: null` observation is consistent with both.

Need empirical capture (Phase 1) to disambiguate.

## The Fix

**Investigation-first**, similar to #10564's Phase-1-before-Phase-3 discipline:

**Phase 1 — Capture (gating evidence):**
- Read the boot lifecycle: `Server.mjs` + `SystemLifecycleService.mjs` to identify whether/where summarization-on-boot is invoked
- If invoked: instrument the call site to log success/error/skip-reason; gather telemetry on next 1–2 boots
- If NOT invoked: identify why — was the call removed, gated behind a flag, or never wired?

**Phase 2 — Targeted intervention:**
Based on Phase 1 evidence:
- If invocation missing: restore the call at boot
- If invocation failing: fix the failure (likely related to the same multi-tenant rollout that produced #10556 — write-side summarization may be tagging records correctly but read-side filter is preventing the summarizer from finding sessions to process)
- If gated: re-enable with appropriate condition

**Phase 3 — Validation:**
- Healthcheck `startup.summarizationStatus` reports `success` after boot on a session-rich corpus
- New session summaries land in `neo-agent-sessions` with `userId: <tenant>` tag
- `runSandman` consumes the new summaries and produces `sandman_handoff.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **(AC1)** Phase 1: empirically determine whether boot-summarization is invoked but failing, OR not invoked at all. Document findings in a comment on this ticket.
- [ ] **(AC2)** If failing: identify root cause (likely interaction with #10556 / #10145 multi-tenant rollout based on circumstantial timing — but verify, don't assume)
- [ ] **(AC3)** Phase 2 intervention shipped: post-boot, `healthcheck.startup.summarizationStatus` returns a non-`not_attempted` value (e.g., `success`, `no_pending_sessions`, or actionable error)
- [ ] **(AC4)** Boot-summarization processes any unsummarized sessions present at boot, tagging new summaries with the active tenant's userId
- [ ] **(AC5)** `runSandman` post-fix produces `sandman_handoff.md` in `resources/content/`
- [ ] **(AC6)** Permanent Playwright test covers the boot-time invocation path (hooks into existing `SessionSummarization.spec.mjs` scaffolding if present)

## Out of Scope

- Sandman daemon scheduling / reliability concerns (separate substrate; this ticket only validates that runSandman gets non-empty input post-fix)
- Multi-tenant write-tagging on summaries (covered by #10556 + the migration runner; if boot-summarization writes are subject to the same tenant logic, this ticket consumes that fix as a precondition)
- Deep DreamService / REM pipeline rework (this ticket targets the missing input, not the downstream consumer)

## Avoided Traps

- **Trap: assume the pipeline is broken without checking whether it's invoked.** Prior fix attempts on Memory Core observability (#10374, #10529, #10017) demonstrate that speculative trigger-refinement without empirical capture is a fragile pattern. Phase 1 gates Phase 2.
- **Trap: bundle with #10556 (chromadb backfill).** Already explicitly rejected per the #10556 Out-of-Scope section. Independent root cause (write-side missing-invocation, vs read-side tenant-filter exclusion).
- **Trap: blame the summarization daemon code without checking the lifecycle invocation.** The most parsimonious explanation per the symptom (`not_attempted` literal status) is that the call site is missing or gated, not that the pipeline implementation is broken.

## Related

- **Adjacent (now MERGED):** #10556 / PR #10567 — chromadb tenant-isolation read-path fix; eliminates one downstream amplifier of summarization staleness
- **Adjacent prior fixes (CLOSED):** #10017 (multi-tenant migration observability), #10374 (sunset trigger refinement), #10529 (sunset disambiguation) — same family of swarm-substrate restoration work
- **Adjacent OPEN:** #10564 (Gemini sunset trigger drift, passive capture)
- **Sandman daemon adjacency:** `buildScripts/ai/runSandman.mjs`, `learn/agentos/DreamPipeline.md`

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-05-01T10:35:41Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Phase 1 capture complete — root cause: intentional config-gate, partially hidden
> 
> The boot-summarization pipeline IS wired and IS code-complete. The reason it doesn't fire is a **config gate that's set to disabled** at two distinct layers:
> 
> ### Layer 1: Template-level gate flipped from default-on to default-opt-in
> 
> `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs` line ~20:
> 
> ```js
> autoSummarize: process.env.AUTO_SUMMARIZE === 'true',
> ```
> 
> Earlier (pre-PR #10042) the template was:
> ```js
> autoSummarize: process.env.AUTO_SUMMARIZE !== 'false',  // default-enabled, opt-out
> ```
> 
> Flipped to default-opt-in via PR [#10042](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10042) ("Implement Local MCP Failsafes, Schema Validation, and IDE Debugging Guides"). Title suggests this was part of a broader "make startup safer / prevent unintended heavy operations on boot" pattern. The flip was **intentional**.
> 
> ### Layer 2: Local config has additional hard-disable
> 
> `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.mjs` (gitignored, copied per-clone) on this canonical instance:
> 
> ```js
> autoSummarize: false && process.env.AUTO_SUMMARIZE !== 'false',
> ```
> 
> The `false && ` prefix short-circuits the env var check entirely — even setting `AUTO_SUMMARIZE=true` would NOT enable it on this instance. This is a hard local override, beyond what the template ships.
> 
> ### How the pipeline IS wired (so we know where it would fire)
> 
> Empirical chain:
> 1. `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/SessionService.mjs:176` — `if (aiConfig.data.autoSummarize)` gates the call
> 2. `:179` — `this.summarizeSessions({}).then(result => ...)` on boot
> 3. `summarizeSessions` (`:664`) is the actual implementation — exposed via MCP as `/sessions/summarize` operationId, also as `summarize_sessions` tool
> 4. Status field `#startupSummarizationStatus` (HealthService.mjs:184) is wired to be set after invocation; never set because the gate's `false`, hence the `not_attempted` literal
> 
> So Phase 1 conclusion: this is **not a bug** in the pipeline. It's a config decision — auto-summarize is disabled by design at both the template level (opt-in) and the canonical instance level (hard-coded false).
> 
> ## Phase 2 decision points for @tobiu
> 
> The fix isn't code — it's policy:
> 
> 1. **Was the local `false && ` deliberate?** Looks suspicious (`false && X` is a code smell suggesting "I meant to comment X out"). If unintentional, remove it and set `AUTO_SUMMARIZE=true` to re-enable.
> 
> 2. **Should the template default be flipped back to opt-out (`!== 'false'`)?** PR #10042's title-level rationale is "failsafes" — likely a transient defensive measure. If the original concern is now resolved (multi-tenant rollout #10145 + write-side tagging stabilized), the default could safely flip back to enabled.
> 
> 3. **Should AGENTS_STARTUP.md §6 be updated?** The doc currently says: *"On startup, the Memory Core server automatically finds and summarizes any previous sessions that were not yet processed."* This is silently false on the canonical instance and on every default-config deployment. Either:
>    - Re-enable auto-summarize and the doc becomes true again
>    - Or update the doc to reflect the opt-in reality + add agent-facing guidance to invoke `summarize_sessions` MCP tool manually if needed
> 
> 4. **Is the Sandman / Golden Path expectation that summaries exist load-bearing?** The downstream `runSandman` failure (no `sandman_handoff.md`) is a direct consequence — the daemon expects summaries to ingest. If auto-summarize stays disabled, runSandman needs to either pull manually-invoked summaries or be restructured to consume raw memories directly.
> 
> ## Adjacent observation
> 
> Dec/Jan-era commits established `autoSummarize: !== 'false'` as default-enabled. Multi-tenant rollout (#10145) was March/April. PR #10042 (the flip) was earlier-than-#10145. So the timing doesn't directly link the flip to multi-tenant concerns — the failsafe motivation was earlier.
> 
> ## My recommendation (subject to @tobiu's call)
> 
> **Path A (lightest):** if `false && ` was unintentional, just remove it. Set `AUTO_SUMMARIZE=true` if you want auto-on. Validate that summarization runs cleanly on next boot. Update AGENTS_STARTUP.md §6 if behavior matches doc claim.
> 
> **Path B (cleaner):** flip template back to `!== 'false'` (default-enabled) since the original failsafe context appears to have aged out. Document the opt-out env var in setup guide. Keep local override as user discretion.
> 
> **Path C (status quo):** accept that auto-summarize is opt-in by design + update AGENTS_STARTUP.md §6 to match. Provide explicit instruction for agents to call `summarize_sessions` on startup if they want session summarization.
> 
> The choice is a maintainer call — directly calls the tradeoff between "magical works-by-default" vs "opt-in safety." I'd lean **Path A first** to investigate the `false && ` provenance before changing template-level policy.
> 
> — Claude Opus 4.7

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-05-01T10:37:57Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## CLOSING — verify-before-assert failure on my part
> 
> @tobiu hard-stopped this ticket with critical context:
> 
> > *"we DISABLED auto boot intentionally. each harness starts 2x+ mcp server instances. massive summary duplication. we have tickets for making mcp servers tiny, moving more into the services sdk, ensuring there is no duplication. URGENT strategy shift. verify before assert failure."*
> 
> The auto-boot disable is **load-bearing strategy**, not a bug:
> - Each harness (Claude Code / Antigravity / Codex) launches **2+ MCP server instances** under typical operating conditions
> - If auto-summarize fired on each, the result would be **massive summary duplication** — every boot of every harness would summarize the same unsummarized sessions, multiplying writes
> - The disable IS the safeguard
> 
> The strategic direction this fits into:
> - **#10186** — MCP concurrency audit + single-writer enforcement (Epic OPEN)
> - **#10103** — SDK-layer config file: migrate daemon/service-layer keys out of memory-core/config.template.mjs (OPEN)
> - **#10063** — Auto-persist turn memories via ai/services.mjs (eliminate manual add_memory) (OPEN)
> 
> The pattern across these: shrink MCP servers, lift logic into the shared services SDK, enforce single-writer on duplication-prone paths. Restoring auto-boot summarization without that consolidation would actively work AGAINST this direction.
> 
> ## Self-correction: my Phase 1 framing was a verify-before-assert failure
> 
> I posted Phase 1 findings + Path A/B/C + a recommendation **without** checking for an existing strategic context for the disable. Per `feedback_verify_before_assert.md`: before asserting that the disable "looks suspicious / could be unintentional / Path A could investigate", I should have run the falsifying tool — searching the open architecture tickets for related work. Two of the three relevant tickets (#10186, #10103) are clearly searchable from "MCP server architecture" / "services SDK" terms I didn't try.
> 
> This is the second verify-before-assert failure today (first was the "legacy precedent" claim earlier on #10556 sentinel naming). Same root pattern: comment-evidence or hypothesis-space generation without grep'ing the existing ticket landscape for adjacent strategy.
> 
> Updating memory accordingly.
> 
> ## Closing as `wontfix` (with strategic-pointer rationale)
> 
> The PROBLEM described in the ticket body (boot-summarization not running) is real. The PROPOSED fix shape (re-enable the gate) is wrong-substrate per the established strategy. The right resolution lives downstream of #10186 + #10103 — once MCP server consolidation lands, single-writer-with-summarization can be re-evaluated.
> 
> For future agents reading this: the `not_attempted` literal in `healthcheck.startup.summarizationStatus` is **expected behavior on the canonical instance**. Do not file a similar fix-shape ticket. If `runSandman` / Golden Path needs summary input in the meantime, the workflow is to invoke `summarize_sessions` MCP tool manually OR run `npm run ai:summarize-sessions` script — not to flip the boot gate.
> 
> Closing.
> 
> — Claude Opus 4.7
